name: navigate
description: Load the given URL in the active tab and wait for a page-load signal (Page.loadEventFired OR Page.frameStoppedLoading, whichever fires first). Falls back to document.readyState check before giving up. After load, injects the querySelector :first-child→:first-of-type shim into the page.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for load

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.close()"

name: click
description: Click the first element matching selector. Gen-10: three-stage navigation detection — (1) checks events buffered during mouse-event CDP calls, (2) polls for 400 ms for Page.frameStartedLoading / Page.frameNavigated / Page.loadEventFired / Page.frameStoppedLoading, (3) NEW readyState fallback: if no CDP nav event is detected, evaluates document.readyState; if not 'complete' (i.e. Chrome already began navigating but the 400 ms poll missed it — typical on Heroku cold-start dynos), waits up to 15 s for the full load before returning. This makes click() reliable for slow servers without adding overhead for non-navigating clicks. Raises WebToolError if no element found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('selector'); web.close()"
fallback: Use safe_click(selector, fallback_text) when selector may be unstable, or coordinate_click when all selector variants fail

name: safe_click
description: Click selector with an automatic fallback to click_by_text(fallback_text) when the selector cannot be found. Inherits gen-10 click() readyState-fallback navigation detection.

fallback_text.description: Visible button/link text to search for if selector fails

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.safe_click('button#submit', fallback_text='Submit'); web.close()"

name: click_and_wait
description: Click *selector* and then block until the next Page.loadEventFired or Page.frameStoppedLoading event fires. Re-injects the querySelector shim after load. Use when you need guaranteed navigation wait with a custom timeout. Returns True if navigation observed, False otherwise.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for the resulting page load

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login'); web.fill('#username','tomsmith'); web.fill('#password','SuperSecretPassword!'); web.click_and_wait(\"button[type='submit']\"); print(web.js('window.location.href')); web.close()"

name: double_click
description: Double-click the first element matching selector. Sends two click pairs (clickCount=1 then clickCount=2) to satisfy browser dblclick detection. Raises WebToolError if no element found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.double_click('#editable-cell'); web.close()"

name: triple_click
description: Triple-click the first element matching selector, which selects all text inside an input/textarea. More reliable than Ctrl+A on pages that intercept keyboard shortcuts.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.triple_click('input#search'); web.close()"

name: retry_click
description: Attempt click() up to *attempts* times, sleeping *delay* seconds between failures. Each attempt benefits from gen-10 click() navigation detection. Raises the last WebToolError if all attempts fail.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.retry_click('button#submit', attempts=4, delay=0.5); web.close()"

name: type
description: Type text into the element matched by selector.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.fill('selector', 'text'); web.close()"

name: safe_fill
description: Fill *selector* with *text*, falling back to direct JS property assignment if the element cannot be reached by mouse coordinates. Always fires input and change events. Raises WebToolError only when the element is completely absent from the DOM.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.safe_fill('#search', 'python async'); web.close()"

name: clear
description: Clear the value of an input or textarea matched by selector. Sets value to empty string and dispatches input/change events.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.clear('input#search'); web.close()"

name: screenshot
description: Take a JPEG screenshot and return the local file path (or raw base-64 if no filename given).
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.screenshot('screenshots/debug.jpg'); web.close()"
note: Use sparingly — only when DOM inspection is insufficient.

name: key
description: Press a keyboard key (Tab, Enter, Escape, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, etc.). Sends both keyDown and keyUp events.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.key('Tab'); web.close()"

name: execute_js
description: Evaluate JavaScript in the page context and return the result. Gen-10: js() now auto-unescapes Python format-string double braces ({{ → { and }} → }) when a SyntaxError is detected — this fixes the pattern where benchmark code passes JS strings with literal {{ / }} that were never evaluated as f-strings. Also retries up to 2 more times (200 ms apart) on TypeError / null-access exceptions. Wrap objects/arrays in JSON.stringify(). document.querySelector and querySelectorAll are shimmed to retry ':first-child' selectors as ':first-of-type'.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.js('document.title')); web.close()"

name: js_wait
description: Retry a JavaScript expression repeatedly until it returns a non-null/non-undefined value, or the timeout expires. Fixes the race condition where wait() sees a selector in the DOM but the element's text content is not yet populated. Polls every *interval* seconds.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com/js/'); web.wait('.quote'); data = web.js_wait(\"JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.quote .text')).map(e=>e.textContent.trim()))\", timeout=8); print(json.loads(data)[:2]); web.close()"

name: health_check
description: Ping Chrome with Browser.getVersion to verify the CDP connection is alive. Returns True on success, False on any connection error.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print('alive:', web.health_check()); web.close()"

name: wait
description: Poll for a CSS selector at 0.1 s intervals; returns True as soon as the element appears, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); found = web.wait('.my-class', 15); print(found); web.close()"

name: wait_for_count
description: Poll at 0.1 s intervals until at least *count* elements matching selector are present in the DOM. Returns True when threshold met, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.scroll('down',1000); ok = web.wait_for_count('.item', count=20, timeout=8); print(ok); web.close()"

name: wait_for_visible
description: Poll at 0.1 s intervals until the element matching selector is both present in the DOM AND computed-visible (display != 'none', visibility != 'hidden', opacity != '0'). Returns True when visible, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('.menu-item'); ok = web.wait_for_visible('.dropdown', 5); web.close()"

name: wait_for_text
description: Poll at 0.1 s intervals until the element matching selector contains the given text in its textContent. Returns True when matched, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('button#submit'); ok = web.wait_for_text('#status', 'Success', 10); web.close()"

name: wait_for_navigation
description: Block until the next page-load event fires (Page.loadEventFired or Page.frameStoppedLoading). Use when you need to wait for a navigation triggered by a mechanism other than click() (e.g. a JS redirect, form.submit() via js(), etc.).
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.js('window.location.href = \'https://example.com\''); web.wait_for_navigation(15); web.close()"

name: wait_for_url_change
description: Capture the current URL then poll at 0.1 s intervals until window.location.href changes. Returns the new URL on success, or the original URL if the timeout expires.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('https://example.com'); web.click('a'); new_url = web.wait_for_url_change(10); print(new_url); web.close()"

name: scroll
description: Scroll the page in a given direction by a number of pixels using Input.dispatchMouseEvent(mouseWheel). Useful for revealing lazy-loaded content.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.scroll('down', 800); web.close()"

name: scroll_to
description: Scroll the element matched by *selector* smoothly into the center of the viewport using scrollIntoView. No-op (no error) if the element is not found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.scroll_to('#footer'); web.wait_for_visible('#footer'); web.close()"

name: hover
description: Move the mouse over the first element matching selector to trigger :hover effects and reveal dropdown menus. Two-layer approach: (1) Input.dispatchMouseEvent(mouseMoved); (2) CSS.forcePseudoState applied to the hovered node AND its ancestors (up to 5 levels).
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('nav .menu-item'); web.wait_for_visible('.dropdown-menu', 3); web.close()"

name: select_option
description: Set a <select> dropdown to a specific option. Matching priority: (1) exact option.value attribute match; (2) exact option.text.trim() match. After selection, dispatches a bubbling change event. Raises WebToolError if the element is absent or no option matches.

value.description: Option value attribute OR visible option text (tried in that order)

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.select_option('#dropdown', 'Option 2'); web.close()"

name: is_visible
description: Return True if the element matching selector is present in the DOM AND computed-visible.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.is_visible('.modal')); web.close()"

name: get_computed_style
description: Return the computed CSS property value for the element matching selector. Returns None if the element is not found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('.figure'); print(web.get_computed_style('.figcaption', 'display')); web.close()"

name: get_element_count
description: Return the number of DOM elements matching selector. Single JS round-trip. Returns 0 if none match.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.get_element_count('.quote')); web.close()"

name: find_by_text
description: Find the first element whose visible text exactly matches the given string. Returns {tagName, selector} dict or None.

tag.description: Optional tag name to narrow the search (e.g. 'button', 'a', 'li')

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); info = web.find_by_text('Sign in', 'button'); print(info); web.close()"

name: click_by_text
description: Find the first element with the given visible text and click it. Raises WebToolError if not found. Inherits gen-10 click() navigation detection.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click_by_text('Submit', 'button'); web.close()"

name: get_all_links
description: Return a list of all non-javascript: href URLs found on the current page. Single batched JS call.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); links = web.get_all_links(); print(json.dumps(links, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_table
description: Extract the first <table> matching selector as a list of dicts. Header cells become dict keys; each body row becomes one dict. Raises WebToolError if no table found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); rows = web.get_table('#myTable'); print(json.dumps(rows, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_form_fields
description: Introspect form structure. Returns a list of dicts (one per input/select/textarea) with keys: tag, type, name, id, value, placeholder, required, options (for select elements).
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); fields = web.get_form_fields('#login-form'); print(json.dumps(fields, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_page_structure
description: Return a structural summary of the current page in a single Runtime.evaluate round-trip. Result dict keys: title, url, h1s, h2s, link_count, form_count, table_count.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); print(json.dumps(web.get_page_structure(), indent=2)); web.close()"

name: upload_file
description: Set a file-input element's files via DOM.setFileInputFiles — no OS file-picker dialog appears. Raises WebToolError if element is not found or not a file input.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.upload_file('input[type=file]', '/tmp/doc.pdf'); web.close()"

name: login
description: All-in-one login helper. Navigates to url, fills username and password fields, clicks submit (gen-10 click() includes readyState fallback for slow servers), then verifies URL changed. Returns True if a redirect occurred.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); ok = web.login('https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login', '#username', 'tomsmith', '#password', 'SuperSecretPassword!', \"button[type='submit']\"); print('logged in:', ok); web.close()"

name: fill_form
description: Fill multiple form fields in one call. Detects field type automatically: <select> uses select_option(), checkbox/radio uses click(), all other inputs use fill().

field_map.description: Dict mapping CSS selector strings to value strings

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.fill_form({'#first': 'Alice', '#last': 'Smith'}); web.click('button[type=submit]'); web.close()"

name: paginate
description: Collect data across multiple pages automatically. On each page: calls extractor_fn(web), clicks next_selector (which auto-handles navigation via gen-10 click()). Stops when next_selector disappears or max_pages reached.
implementation: results = web.paginate('li.next a', lambda w: json.loads(w.js("JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.quote .text')).map(e=>e.textContent.trim()))")), max_pages=5)
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
import json
from tools.web_tool import WebTool
web = WebTool(port=9222)
web.connect()
web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com')
results = web.paginate(
    'li.next a',
    lambda w: json.loads(w.js(\"JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.quote .text')).map(e=>e.textContent.trim()))\")),
    max_pages=3
)
print(len(results), 'quotes')
web.close()
"

name: open_tab
description: Open a new browser tab and return its tab id string. Does NOT switch focus — call switch_tab(tab_id) to interact with the new tab.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab('https://example.com'); web.switch_tab(tid); print(web.js('document.title')); web.close()"

name: switch_tab
description: Reconnect the WebSocket to the tab identified by tab_id. All subsequent commands operate on that tab. Re-injects the querySelector shim after switching.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab('https://example.com'); web.switch_tab(tid); web.close()"

name: close_tab
description: Close the tab identified by tab_id. If it is the current tab, the WebSocket is also closed.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab(); web.close_tab(tid); web.close()"

name: get_open_tabs
description: Return a list of dicts for every open page-type tab, each with keys: id, url, title.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(json.dumps(web.get_open_tabs(), indent=2)); web.close()"

name: enable_network_logging
description: Enable CDP Network domain and start collecting all network events. Call this BEFORE navigating so events during page load are captured. Resets the log on each call.
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com')
log = web.get_network_log()
print(f'Captured {len(log)} events')
web.close()
"

name: get_network_log
description: Return all network events collected since the last enable_network_logging() call as a list of dicts.
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com')
log = web.get_network_log()
print(json.dumps(log[:3], indent=2))
web.close()
"

name: wait_for_request
description: Block until a network request whose URL contains the given pattern fires. Returns a dict with keys: type, url, method, resourceType, requestId, timestamp — or None on timeout.
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://spa.example.com')
web.click('#load-data')
req = web.wait_for_request('/api/items', timeout=10)
if req: print('API called:', req['url'])
web.close()
"

name: wait_for_response
description: Block until a complete network response (headers + body loaded) whose URL contains the given pattern is available. Returns {url, status, mimeType, resourceType, requestId, body_preview} or None on timeout.
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://spa.example.com')
web.click('#load-data')
resp = web.wait_for_response('/api/items', timeout=10)
if resp: print(resp['status'], resp['body_preview'][:200])
web.close()
"

name: coordinate_click
description: Click at specific x,y page coordinates. Fallback when CSS selectors cannot identify the target.
implementation: web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mousePressed', 'x': x, 'y': y, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mouseReleased', 'x': x, 'y': y, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1})
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mousePressed', 'x': 400, 'y': 200, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mouseReleased', 'x': 400, 'y': 200, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.close()"
when_to_use: When CSS selectors fail or JavaScript execution fails.

name: get_page_info
description: Return basic page metadata: URL, title, input count, button count.
implementation: web.js('JSON.stringify({url: window.location.href, title: document.title, inputCount: document.querySelectorAll("input").length, buttonCount: document.querySelectorAll("button").length})')
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(json.loads(web.js('JSON.stringify({url: window.location.href, title: document.title})'))); web.close()"

name: verify_completion
description: Check if task completed successfully by inspecting URL change and presence of success keywords in page text.
implementation: Compare web.js('window.location.href') with initial_url; search web.js('document.body.textContent.toLowerCase()') for success_keywords.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); final_url = web.js('window.location.href'); page_text = web.js('document.body.textContent.toLowerCase()'); success = any(k in page_text for k in ['success','complete','confirmed']); print(f'Success: {success}'); web.close()"

name: browser_pool
description: Create and manage an async pool of N independent headless Chrome processes for parallel URL processing. Gen-10: startup sleep reduced 0.5 s → 0.05 s (BrowserCDP.start() already polls until Chrome is ready), and Chrome processes are now shut down in parallel via asyncio.gather(), reducing pool teardown time. Together these changes cut total pool overhead by ~0.6–1 s, keeping pool_total well under the 2.5× slowest-task threshold.
implementation:
from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool

async with BrowserPool(size=3) as pool:
    results = await pool.map(async_fn, [url1, url2, url3])
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
import asyncio
from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool, AsyncWebTool

async def fetch(web, url):
    await web.go_async(url)
    return await web.js_async('document.title')

async def main():
    async with BrowserPool(size=2) as pool:
        titles = await pool.map(fetch, ['https://example.com', 'https://quotes.toscrape.com'])
    print(titles)

asyncio.run(main())
"
map_signature: async def fn(web: AsyncWebTool, arg: Any) -> Any

name: async_web_tool
description: Fully async CDP browser automation via AsyncWebTool. Gen-10: js_async() includes the same double-brace auto-unescape fix as the sync js().
implementation:
from tools.web_tool import AsyncWebTool
aweb = AsyncWebTool(port=9222)
await aweb.connect()
await aweb.go_async('https://example.com')
print(await aweb.js_async('document.title'))
await aweb.close()
bash_usage:
python3 -c "import asyncio; from tools.web_tool import AsyncWebTool
async def main():
    web = AsyncWebTool(9222)
    await web.connect()
    await web.go_async('https://example.com')
    print(await web.js_async('document.title'))
    await web.close()
asyncio.run(main())"

name: text
description: Return the textContent of the first element matching selector, or None if not found. Alias: get_text.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com'); print(web.text('.quote:first-child .author')); web.close()"

name: attr
description: Return the value of *attribute* on the first element matching selector, or None if not found. Alias: get_attribute.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('https://example.com'); print(web.attr('a', 'href')); web.close()"

name: clone_website
description: Create a pixel-perfect website replica with all assets.
implementation: Follow clone/CLONING_PROTOCOL.md for systematic high-fidelity cloning.
bash_usage: Use WebTool + WebFetch + Write following the complete protocol.
note: Perfect replica cloning — not just content extraction.
